raw_consumer_statement:
The Background : Back in XXXX, I booked a " XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX  '' via XXXX. Ive used XXXX before ( including a trip to XXXX recently ) and it worked fine, so I didn't suspect anything. 
XX/XX/XXXX : I was charged {$3700.00} by " Golden Days. '' XX/XX/XXXX : Received a cancellation notice from XXXX, but it told me to contact the property manager at a specific email address to resolve it. 
XX/XX/XXXX XX/XX/XXXX : The " manager '' claimed it was a " system glitch '' and told me the reservation was still good. 
XX/XX/XXXX : I tried to follow up again. The email bounced, the phone number was unreachable. The company vanished. The host is gone. 
The Dispute : I contacted Booking.com and they told me I had to file a dispute through my credit card. I did. Citi has now denied it three times. 

Citi says they don't take phone calls or discuss the considerations of this matter. They seem to ignore the paper trail by first saying the date of service had not passed and then losing or disconnecting the information from the case so that I have had to provide documentation again. This seems to be a way that they have dragged out the determination in this case. This is a case of services not received because the merchant is a bad actor that used a major platform to charge me for nearly {$4000.00} and then disappeared. It is a clear case of services not received.
